methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
CubeSpace is a Growth-stage company in Space & Aerospace founded by Mike-Alec Kearney. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace—a total of 13 direct sector peers.

Two standard triangulation methods do not yield reliable results here. Peer-median analysis is inapplicable: while 23 same-sector private peers exist, the subset meeting narrow stage and valuation-tier filters contains fewer than the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical validity. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot proceed because CubeSpace's revenue is not on file; this is typical for private companies at the Growth stage. The Space & Aerospace public median P/S stands at 64.2x across the 6 tickers, but application requires disclosed target revenue.

Given data constraints, no defensible triangulated valuation range can be calculated. The analysis is limited to observational context: CubeSpace operates in a sector where public multiples are elevated (64.2x P/S median), and private peers span a wide range from well-capitalized incumbents (SpaceX, Blue Origin) to recently public growth companies (Rocket Lab, Planet Labs). A credible valuation would require either (1) disclosure of CubeSpace's revenue and growth trajectory, (2) expansion of disclosed financials from private comparables, or (3) qualitative positioning relative to named peers on development stage and commercialization timeline.
